description_long: This object is a traditional water well structure featuring a cylindrical bucket or barrel suspended within a square wooden frame. The well is topped with a pitched roof supported by two vertical posts, providing shelter from the elements. A hand crank mechanism with a visible wheel is attached to one side, allowing the bucket to be raised or lowered into the well shaft. The base of the structure is surrounded by a flat, square platform, likely representing a paved or wooden surface for stability. The bucket itself appears to be made of dark metal or wood, and the overall construction uses a combination of wood and metal elements. The design is reminiscent of classic wells found in parks, gardens, or rural urban settings.
description: A classic water well with a pitched roof, hand crank, and suspended bucket, set on a square platform. The structure combines wood and metal elements for a traditional appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the square roof, bucket opening, and partial view of the crank mechanism on the right.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the pitched roof, the wheel of the crank mechanism, and the open well shaft.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, with the roof and bucket opening visible, but the crank mechanism is less prominent.
description_view_3: Rear angled view showing the crank wheel and the open well shaft beneath the pitched roof.
